title: A Comparative Analysis of Relational Database Access-Layer Performance in Express.js across PostgreSQL and MySQL
description: Replication package (benchmark harness + paper sources + raw results) for a neutral comparison of relational database access layers in an Express.js service: native drivers (pg, mysql2), a query builder (Knex), and six ORMs (Drizzle, Prisma, Sequelize, TypeORM, Objection, MikroORM), across PostgreSQL 18.4 and MySQL 9.7.1, over five access patterns (point read, keyset range scan, deep/nested fetch, aggregation, insert), reporting throughput (req/s) and tail latency (p50/p90/p97.5/p99) jointly. Protocol: 25 independent replicates per cell (fresh server process per replicate, randomized cell order, 12 s runs after warm-up), connection pool fixed at 10; automated cross-layer correctness cross-check; captured per-layer SQL, round-trip counts, and EXPLAIN plans. Supplementary runs: same-plan deep-fetch control, open-loop (constant-arrival) tail validation, three durability regimes, pinned-core resource separation, ten-minute sustained-load drift check, and a no-database Express baseline. One-command re-run against containerized (digest-pinned) or local engines.

creators.name: Miotk, Mateusz
creators.affiliation: Faculty of Mathematics, Physics and Informatics, University of Gdańsk
